verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचनन्दव्रजं गते रामे करूषाधिपतिर्नृप ।वासुदेवोऽहमित्यज्ञो दूतं कृष्णाय प्राहिणोत् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācananda-vrajaṁ gate rāmekarūṣādhipatir nṛpavāsudevo ’ham ity ajñodūtaṁ kṛṣṇāya prāhiṇot
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, while Lord Balarāma was away visiting Nanda’s village of Vraja, the ruler of Karūṣa, foolishly thinking “I am the Supreme Lord, Vāsudeva,” sent a messenger to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं वासुदेवो भगवानवतीर्णो जगत्पति: ।इति प्रस्तोभितो बालैर्मेन आत्मानमच्युतम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tvaṁ vāsudevo bhagavānavatīṛno jagat-patiḥiti prastobhito bālairmena ātmānam acyutam
verses.text: Pauṇḍraka was emboldened by the flattery of childish men, who told him, “You are Vāsudeva, the Supreme Lord and master of the universe, who have now descended to the earth.” Thus he imagined himself to be the infallible Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: दूतं च प्राहिणोन्मन्द: कृष्णायाव्यक्तवर्त्मने ।द्वारकायां यथा बालो नृपो बालकृतोऽबुध: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dūtaṁ ca prāhiṇon mandaḥkṛṣṇāyāvyakta-vartmanedvārakāyāṁ yathā bālonṛpo bāla-kṛto ’budhaḥ
verses.text: Thus slow-witted King Pauṇḍraka sent a messenger to the inscrutable Lord Kṛṣṇa at Dvārakā. Pauṇḍraka was acting just like an unintelligent child whom other children are pretending is a king.

verses.sanskrit: दूतस्तु द्वारकामेत्य सभायामास्थितं प्रभुम् ।कृष्णं कमलपत्राक्षं राजसन्देशमब्रवीत् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: dūtas tu dvārakām etyasabhāyām āsthitaṁ prabhumkṛṣṇaṁ kamala-patrākṣaṁrāja-sandeśam abravīt
verses.text: Arriving in Dvārakā, the messenger found lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa in His royal assembly and relayed the King’s message to that almighty Lord.

verses.sanskrit: वासुदेवोऽवतीर्णोऽहमेक एव न चापर: ।भूतानामनुकम्पार्थं त्वं तु मिथ्याभिधांत्यज ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vāsudevo ’vatīrno ’hameka eva na cāparaḥbhūtānām anukampārthaṁtvaṁ tu mithyābhidhāṁ tyaja
verses.text: [On Pauṇḍraka’s behalf, the messenger said:] I am the one and only Lord Vāsudeva, and there is no other. It is I who have descended to this world to show mercy to the living beings. Therefore give up Your false name.

verses.sanskrit: यानि त्वमस्मच्चिह्नानि मौढ्याद् बिभर्षि सात्वत ।त्यक्त्वैहि मां त्वं शरणंनोचेद् देहि ममाहवम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yāni tvam asmac-cihnānimauḍhyād bibharṣi sātvatatyaktvaihi māṁ tvaṁ śaraṇaṁno ced dehi mamāhavam
verses.text: O Sātvata, give up my personal symbols, which out of foolishness You now carry, and come to me for shelter. If You do not, then You must give me battle.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचकत्थनं तदुपाकर्ण्य पौण्ड्रकस्याल्पमेधस: ।उग्रसेनादय: सभ्या उच्चकैर्जहसुस्तदा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācakatthanaṁ tad upākarṇyapauṇḍrakasyālpa-medhasaḥugrasenādayaḥ sabhyāuccakair jahasus tadā
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: King Ugrasena and the other members of the assembly laughed loudly when they heard this vain boasting of unintelligent Pauṇḍraka.

verses.sanskrit: उवाच दूतं भगवान् परिहासकथामनु ।उत्स्रक्ष्ये मूढ चिह्नानि यैस्त्वमेवं विकत्थसे ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: uvāca dūtaṁ bhagavānparihāsa-kathām anuutsrakṣye mūḍha cihnāniyais tvam evaṁ vikatthase
verses.text: The Personality of Godhead, after enjoying the jokes of the assembly, told the messenger [to relay a message to his master:] “You fool, I will indeed let loose the weapons you boast of in this way.

verses.sanskrit: मुखं तदपिधायाज्ञ कङ्कगृध्रवटैर्वृत: ।शयिष्यसे हतस्तत्र भविता शरणं शुनाम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: mukhaṁ tad apidhāyājñakaṅka-gṛdhra-vaṭair vṛtaḥśayiṣyase hatas tatrabhavitā śaraṇaṁ śunām
verses.text: “When you lie dead, O fool, your face covered by vultures, herons and vaṭa birds, you will become the shelter of dogs.”

verses.sanskrit: इति दूतस्तमाक्षेपं स्वामिने सर्वमाहरत् ।कृष्णोऽपि रथमास्थाय काशीमुपजगाम ह ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: iti dūtas tam ākṣepaṁsvāmine sarvam āharatkṛṣṇo ’pi ratham āsthāyakāśīm upajagāma ha
verses.text: When the Lord had thus spoken, the messenger conveyed His insulting reply to his master in its entirety. Lord Kṛṣṇa then mounted His chariot and went to the vicinity of Kāśī.

verses.sanskrit: पौण्ड्रकोऽपि तदुद्योगमुपलभ्य महारथ: ।अक्षौहिणीभ्यां संयुक्तो निश्चक्राम पुराद् द्रुतम् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: pauṇḍrako ’pi tad-udyogamupalabhya mahā-rathaḥakṣauhiṇībhyāṁ saṁyuktoniścakrāma purād drutam
verses.text: Upon observing Lord Kṛṣṇa’s preparations for battle, the mighty warrior Pauṇḍraka quickly went out of the city with two full military divisions.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य काशीपतिर्मित्रं पार्ष्णिग्राहोऽन्वयान्नृप ।अक्षौहिणीभिस्तिसृभिरपश्यत् पौण्ड्रकं हरि: ॥ १२ ॥शङ्खार्यसिगदाशार्ङ्गश्रीवत्साद्युपलक्षितम् ।बिभ्राणं कौस्तुभमणिं वनमालाविभूषितम् ॥ १३ ॥कौशेयवाससी पीते वसानं गरुडध्वजम् ।अमूल्यमौल्याभरणं स्फुरन्मकरकुण्डलम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya kāśī-patir mitraṁpārṣṇi-grāho ’nvayān nṛpaakṣauhiṇībhis tisṛbhirapaśyat pauṇḍrakaṁ hariḥ
verses.text: Pauṇḍraka’s friend, the King of Kāśī, followed behind, O King, leading the rear guard with three akṣauhiṇī divisions. Lord Kṛṣṇa saw that Pauṇḍraka was carrying the Lord’s own insignia, such as the conchshell, disc, sword and club, and also an imitation Śārṅga bow and Śrīvatsa mark. He wore a mock Kaustubha gem, was decorated with a garland of forest flowers and was dressed in upper and lower garments of fine yellow silk. His banner bore the image of Garuḍa, and he wore a valuable crown and gleaming, shark-shaped earrings.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा तमात्मनस्तुल्यं वेषं कृत्रिममास्थितम् ।यथा नटं रङ्गगतं विजहास भृशं हरि: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tam ātmanas tulyaṁveṣaṁ kṛtrimam āsthitamyathā naṭaṁ raṅga-gataṁvijahāsa bhṛśaṁ harīḥ
verses.text: Lord Hari laughed heartily when He saw how the King had dressed up in exact imitation of His own appearance, just like an actor onstage.

verses.sanskrit: शूलैर्गदाभि: परिघै: शक्त्यृष्टिप्रासतोमरै: ।असिभि: पट्टिशैर्बाणै: प्राहरन्नरयो हरिम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śulair gadābhiḥ parighaiḥśakty-ṛṣṭi-prāsa-tomaraiḥasibhiḥ paṭṭiśair bāṇaiḥprāharann arayo harim
verses.text: The enemies of Lord Hari attacked Him with tridents, clubs, bludgeons, pikes, ṛṣtis, barbed darts, lances, swords, axes and arrows.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णस्तु तत्पौण्ड्रककाशिराजयो-र्बलं गजस्यन्दनवाजिपत्तिमत् ।गदासिचक्रेषुभिरार्दयद् भृशंयथा युगान्ते हढतभुक् पृथक् प्रजा: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇas tu tat pauṇḍraka-kāśirājayorbalaṁ gaja-syandana-vāji-patti-matgadāsi-cakreṣubhir ārdayad bhṛśaṁyathā yugānte huta-bhuk pṛthak prajāḥ
verses.text: But Lord Kṛṣṇa fiercely struck back at the army of Pauṇḍraka and Kāśirāja, which consisted of elephants, chariots, cavalry and infantry. The Lord tormented His enemies with His club, sword, Sudarśana disc and arrows, just as the fire of annihilation torments the various kinds of creatures at the end of a cosmic age.

verses.sanskrit: आयोधनं तद्रथवाजिकुञ्जर-द्विपत्खरोष्ट्रैररिणावखण्डितै: ।बभौ चितं मोदवहं मनस्विना-माक्रीडनं भूतपतेरिवोल्बणम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: āyodhanaṁ tad ratha-vāji-kuñjara-dvipat-kharoṣṭrair ariṇāvakhaṇḍitaiḥbabhau citaṁ moda-vahaṁ manasvināmākrīḍanaṁ bhūta-pater ivolbaṇam
verses.text: The battlefield, strewn with the dismembered chariots, horses, elephants, humans, mules and camels that had been cut to pieces by the Lord’s disc weapon, shone like the gruesome playground of Lord Bhūtapati, giving pleasure to the wise.

verses.sanskrit: अथाह पौण्ड्रकं शौरिर्भो भो पौण्ड्रक यद् भवान् ।दूतवाक्येन मामाह तान्यस्‍त्रण्युत्सृजामि ते ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāha pauṇḍrakaṁ śaurirbho bho pauṇḍraka yad bhavāndūta-vākyena mām āhatāny astraṇy utsṛjāmi te
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa then addressed Pauṇḍraka: My dear Pauṇḍraka, the very weapons you spoke of through your messenger, I now release unto you.

verses.sanskrit: त्याजयिष्येऽभिधानं मे यत्त्वयाज्ञ मृषा धृतम् ।व्रजामि शरनं तेऽद्य यदि नेच्छामि संयुगम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tyājayiṣye ’bhidhānaṁ meyat tvayājña mṛṣā dhṛtamvrajāmi śaranaṁ te ’dyayadi necchāmi saṁyugam
verses.text: O fool, now I shall make you renounce My name, which you have falsely assumed. And I will certainly take shelter of you if I do not wish to fight you.

verses.sanskrit: इति क्षिप्‍त्‍वा शितैर्बाणैर्विरथीकृत्य पौण्ड्रकम् ।शिरोऽवृश्चद् रथाङ्गेन वज्रेणेन्द्रो यथा गिरे: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti kṣiptvā śitair bāṇairvirathī-kṛtya pauṇḍrakamśiro ’vṛścad rathāṅgenavajreṇendro yathā gireḥ
verses.text: Having thus derided Pauṇḍraka, Lord Kṛṣṇa destroyed his chariot with His sharp arrows. The Lord then cut off his head with the Sudarśana disc, just as Lord Indra lops off a mountain peak with his thunderbolt weapon.

verses.sanskrit: तथा काशिपते: कायाच्छिर उत्कृत्य पत्रिभि: ।न्यपातयत् काशिपुर्यां पद्मकोशमिवानिल: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathā kāśī-pateḥ kāyācchira utkṛtya patribhiḥnyapātayat kāśī-puryāṁpadma-kośam ivānilaḥ
verses.text: With His arrows, Lord Kṛṣṇa similarly severed Kāśirāja’s head from his body, sending it flying into Kāśī city like a lotus flower thrown by the wind.

verses.sanskrit: एवं मत्सरिणं हत्वा पौण्ड्रकं ससखं हरि: ।द्वारकामाविशत् सिद्धैर्गीयमानकथामृत: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ matsariṇam hatvāpauṇḍrakaṁ sa-sakhaṁ hariḥdvārakām āviśat siddhairgīyamāna-kathāmṛtaḥ
verses.text: Having thus killed envious Pauṇḍraka and his ally, Lord Kṛṣṇa returned to Dvārakā. As He entered the city, the Siddhas of heaven chanted His immortal, nectarean glories.

verses.sanskrit: स नित्यं भगवद्ध्यानप्रध्वस्ताखिलबन्धन: ।बिभ्राणश्च हरे राजन् स्वरूपं तन्मयोऽभवत् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa nityaṁ bhagavad-dhyāna-pradhvastākhila-bandhanaḥbibhrāṇaś ca hare rājansvarūpaṁ tan-mayo ’bhavat
verses.text: By constantly meditating upon the Supreme Lord, Pauṇḍraka shattered all his material bonds. Indeed, by imitating Lord Kṛṣṇa’s appearance, O King, he ultimately became Kṛṣṇa conscious.

verses.sanskrit: शिर: पतितमालोक्य राजद्वारे सकुण्डलम् ।किमिदं कस्य वा वक्त्रमिति संशिशिरे जना: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śiraḥ patitam ālokyarāja-dvāre sa-kuṇḍalamkim idaṁ kasya vā vaktramiti saṁśiśire janāḥ
verses.text: Seeing a head decorated with earrings lying at the gate of the royal palace, the people present were puzzled. Some of them asked, “What is this?” and others said, “It is a head, but whose is it?”

verses.sanskrit: राज्ञ: काशीपतेर्ज्ञात्वा महिष्य: पुत्रबान्धवा: ।पौराश्च हा हता राजन् नाथ नाथेति प्रारुदन् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājñaḥ kāśī-pater jñātvāmahiṣyaḥ putra-bāndhavāḥpaurāś ca hā hatā rājannātha nātheti prārudan
verses.text: My dear King, when they recognized it as the head of their King — the lord of Kāśi — his queens, sons and other relatives, along with all the citizens of the city, began to cry pitifully: “Alas, we are killed! O my lord, my lord!”

verses.sanskrit: सुदक्षिणस्तस्य सुत: कृत्वा संस्थाविधिं पते: ।निहत्य पितृहन्तारं यास्याम्यपचितिं पितु: ॥ २७ ॥इत्यात्मनाभिसन्धाय सोपाध्यायो महेश्वरम् ।सुदक्षिणोऽर्चयामास परमेण समाधिना ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sudakṣiṇas tasya sutaḥkṛtvā saṁsthā-vidhiṁ pateḥnihatya pitṛ-hantāraṁyāsyāmy apacitiṁ pituḥ
verses.text: After the King’s son Sudakṣiṇa had performed the obligatory funeral rituals for his father, he resolved within his mind: “Only by killing my father’s murderer can I avenge his death.” Thus the charitable Sudakṣiṇa, together with his priests, began worshiping Lord Maheśvara with great attention.

verses.sanskrit: प्रीतोऽविमुक्ते भगवांस्तस्मै वरमदाद् विभु: ।पितृहन्तृवधोपायं स वव्रे वरमीप्सितम् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: prīto ’vimukte bhagavāṁstasmai varam adād vibhuḥpitṛ-hantṛ-vadhopāyaṁsa vavre varam īpsitam
verses.text: Satisfied by the worship, the powerful Lord Śiva appeared in the sacred precinct of Avimukta and offered Sudakṣiṇa his choice of benedictions. The prince chose as his benediction a means to slay his father’s killer.

verses.sanskrit: दक्षिणाग्निं परिचर ब्राह्मणै: सममृत्विजम् ।अभिचारविधानेन स चाग्नि: प्रमथैर्वृत: ॥ ३० ॥साधयिष्यति सङ्कल्पमब्रह्मण्ये प्रयोजित: ।इत्यादिष्टस्तथा चक्रे कृष्णायाभिचरन् व्रती ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: dakṣiṇāgniṁ paricarabrāhmaṇaiḥ samam ṛtvijamabhicāra-vidhānenasa cāgniḥ pramathair vṛtaḥ
verses.text: Lord Śiva told him, “Accompanied by brāhmaṇas, serve the Dakṣiṇāgni fire — the original priest — following the injunctions of the abhicāra ritual. Then the Dakṣiṇāgni fire, together with many Pramathas, will fulfill your desire if you direct it against someone inimical to the brāhmaṇas.” So instructed, Sudakṣiṇa strictly observed the ritualistic vows and invoked the abhicāra against Lord Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: ततोऽग्निरुत्थित: कुण्डान्मूर्तिमानतिभीषण: ।तप्तताम्रशिखाश्मश्रुरङ्गारोद्गारिलोचन: ॥ ३२ ॥दंष्ट्रोग्रभ्रुकुटीदण्डकठोरास्य: स्वजिह्वया ।आलिहन् सृक्व‍णी नग्नो विधुन्वंस्‍त्रिशिखं ज्वलत् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’gnir utthitaḥ kuṇḍānmūrtimān ati-bhīṣaṇaḥtapta-tāmra-śikhā-śmaśruraṅgārodgāri-locanaḥ
verses.text: Thereupon the fire rose up out of the altar pit, assuming the form of an extremely fearsome, naked person. The fiery creature’s beard and tuft of hair were like molten copper, and his eyes emitted blazing hot cinders. His face looked most frightful with its fangs and terrible arched and furrowed brows. As he licked the corners of his mouth with his tongue, the demon shook his flaming trident.

verses.sanskrit: पद्‍भ्यां तालप्रमाणाभ्यां कम्पयन्नवनीतलम् ।सोऽभ्यधावद् वृतो भूतैर्द्वारकां प्रदहन् दिश: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: padbhyāṁ tāla-pramāṇābhyāṁkampayann avanī-talamso ’bhyadhāvad vṛto bhūtairdvārakāṁ pradahan diśaḥ
verses.text: On legs as tall as palm trees, the monster raced toward Dvārakā in the company of ghostly spirits, shaking the ground and burning the world in all directions.

verses.sanskrit: तमाभिचारदहनमायान्तं द्वारकौकस: ।विलोक्य तत्रसु: सर्वे वनदाहे मृगा यथा ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam ābhicāra-dahanamāyāntaṁ dvārakaukasaḥvilokya tatrasuḥ sarvevana-dāhe mṛgā yathā
verses.text: Seeing the approacḥ of the fiery demon created by the abhicāra ritual, the residents of Dvārakā were all struck with fear, like animals terrified by a forest fire.

verses.sanskrit: अक्षै: सभायां क्रीडन्तं भगवन्तं भयातुरा: ।त्राहि त्राहि त्रिलोकेश वह्ने: प्रदहत: पुरम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: akṣaiḥ sabhāyāṁ krīḍantaṁbhagavantaṁ bhayāturāḥtrāhi trāhi tri-lokeśavahneḥ pradahataḥ puram
verses.text: Distraught with fear, the people cried out to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who was then playing at dice in the royal court: “Save us! Save us, O Lord of the three worlds, from this fire burning up the city!”

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तज्जनवैक्लव्यं द‍ृष्ट्वा स्वानां च साध्वसम् ।शरण्य: सम्प्रहस्याह मा भैष्टेत्यवितास्म्यहम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutvā taj jana-vaiklavyaṁdṛṣṭvā svānāṁ ca sādhvasamśaraṇyaḥ samprahasyāhamā bhaiṣṭety avitāsmy aham
verses.text: When Lord Kṛṣṇa heard the people’s agitation and saw that even His own men were disturbed, that most worthy giver of shelter simply laughed and told them, “Do not fear; I shall protect you.”

verses.sanskrit: सर्वस्यान्तर्बहि:साक्षी कृत्यां माहेश्वरीं विभु: ।विज्ञाय तद्विघातार्थं पार्श्वस्थं चक्रमादिशत् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarvasyāntar-bahiḥ-sākṣīkṛtyāṁ māheśvarīṁ vibhuḥvijñāya tad-vighātārthaṁpārśva-sthaṁ cakram ādiśat
verses.text: The almighty Lord, the internal and external witness of all, understood that the monster had been produced by Lord Śiva from the sacrificial fire. To defeat the demon, Kṛṣṇa dispatched His disc weapon, who was waiting at His side.

verses.sanskrit: तत् सूर्यकोटिप्रतिमं सुदर्शनंजाज्वल्यमानं प्रलयानलप्रभम् ।स्वतेजसा खं ककुभोऽथ रोदसीचक्रं मुकुन्दास्‍त्रमथाग्निमार्दयत् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat sūrya-koṭi-pratimaṁ sudarśanaṁjājvalyamānaṁ pralayānala-prabhamsva-tejasā khaṁ kakubho ’tha rodasīcakraṁ mukundāstraṁ athāgnim ārdayat
verses.text: That Sudarśana, the disc weapon of Lord Mukunda, blazed forth like millions of suns. His effulgence blazed like the fire of universal annihilation, and with his heat he pained the sky, all the directions, heaven and earth, and also the fiery demon.

verses.sanskrit: कृत्यानल: प्रतिहत: स रथाङ्गपाणे-रस्त्रौजसा स नृप भग्नमुखो निवृत्त: ।वाराणसीं परिसमेत्य सुदक्षिणं तंसर्त्विग्जनं समदहत् स्वकृतोऽभिचार: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛtyānalaḥ pratihataḥ sa rathānga-pāṇerastraujasā sa nṛpa bhagna-mukho nivṛttaḥvārāṇasīṁ parisametya sudakṣiṇaṁ taṁsartvig-janaṁ samadahat sva-kṛto ’bhicāraḥ
verses.text: Frustrated by the power of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s weapon, O King, the fiery creature produced by black magic turned his face away and retreated. Created for violence, the demon then returned to Vārāṇasī, where he surrounded the city and then burned Sudakṣiṇa and his priests to death, even though Sudakṣiṇa was his creator.

verses.sanskrit: चक्रं च विष्णोस्तदनुप्रविष्टंवाराणसीं साट्टसभालयापणाम् ।सगोपुराट्टालककोष्ठसङ्कुलांसकोशहस्त्यश्वरथान्नशालिनीम् ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: cakraṁ ca viṣṇos tad-anupraviṣṭaṁvārānasīṁ sāṭṭa-sabhālayāpaṇāmsa-gopurāṭṭālaka-koṣṭha-saṅkulāṁsa-kośa-hasty-aśva-rathānna-śālinīm
verses.text: Lord Viṣṇu’s disc also entered Vārāṇasī, in pursuit of the fiery demon, and proceeded to burn the city to the ground, including all its assembly halls and residential palaces with raised porches, its numerous marketplaces, gateways, watchtowers, warehouses and treasuries, and all the buildings housing elephants, horses, chariots and grains.

verses.sanskrit: दग्ध्वा वाराणसीं सर्वां विष्णोश्चक्रं सुदर्शनम् ।भूय: पार्श्वमुपातिष्ठत् कृष्णस्याक्लिष्टकर्मण: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: dagdhvā vārāṇasīṁ sarvāṁviṣṇoś cakraṁ sudarśanambhūyaḥ pārśvam upātiṣṭhatkṛṣṇasyākliṣṭa-karmaṇaḥ
verses.text: After burning down the entire city of Vārāṇasī, Lord Viṣṇu’s Sudarśana cakra returned to the side of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, whose actions are effortless.

verses.sanskrit: य एनं श्रावयेन्मर्त्य उत्तम:श्लोकविक्रमम् ।समाहितो वा श‍ृणुयात् सर्वपापै: प्रमुच्यते ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ya enaṁ śrāvayen martyauttamaḥ-śloka-vikramamsamāhito vā śṛṇuyātsarva-pāpaiḥ pramucyate
verses.text: Any mortal who recounts this heroic pastime of Lord Uttamaḥ-śloka’s, or who simply hears it attentively, will become freed from all sins.
